rag_rules: Place assume/guarantee declarations in component type scopes for interface behavior; use implementation scopes for subcomponent bindings and assignments.
Use `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks to contain AGREE contract elements.
generic_rules: Refer to component ports only through valid variables or outputs in component type contracts.
Declare constants and equations in component type annexes when describing interface behavior.
Use visible identifiers and feature names to define bounds and logical relationships in AGREE contracts.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE syntax includes `assume`, `guarantee`, `eq`, `const`, and `assign` statements within `annex agree` blocks.
Component type contracts should define assumptions and guarantees using named constants or visible feature relations.
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-level assignments in component type scopes.
Avoid referencing AADL properties like Period or Deadline directly in AGREE expressions without explicit typed ports.
irrelevant_topics: AADL property associations and timing parameters not directly tied to visible features
